prompt: I have a broad plan for an AI assistant that can act on customer accounts. Route the review without assuming the method review replaces security or legal review.
Review an autonomous-agent launch that has no tested rollback and close the loop. Keep the review bounded and say what outside expertise remains necessary.
A team wants to automate a weekly report with twelve fields, three approvals, and five notification channels. Review the plan before implementation.
We assume a customer dashboard must update in real time because competitors do. Rebuild the decision from facts and constraints.
A manager wants to remove an employee for low ownership but has no documented examples, and the role has changed twice. Review the team problem.
I want to quit my job tomorrow for an untested startup, but I have dependents and two months of savings. Review the commitment decision.
Review a medical triage agent that can autonomously deny care, has no audit log, and cannot be rolled back after deployment.
Give me a short reading path for first-principles engineering and distinguish books directly attributed to Musk from your own recommendations.

A local plugin cache contains every expected folder, but several commands do not appear in the model prompt. Diagnose the distinction and give one targeted next command without changing global configuration.
I am considering spending six months building a copy of an ad-arbitrage product solely because a competitor raised money. Review whether the project deserves pursuit.

A hardware team wants to buy an expensive sealed sensor module without comparing component costs or whether its enclosure, firmware, and assembly should be designed together. Review the engineering decision.
A release needs seven status meetings and four approvals, including one security signoff for production credentials. Simplify the organization path without removing controls that prevent real harm.

A migration is six weeks late because independent work is serialized, nobody owns the critical path, and the team tracks twelve metrics. Compress the timeline without hiding uncertainty or rollback needs.

A deployment pipeline spends forty minutes waiting for one flaky integration suite while build, review, and packaging are fast. Treat this as a production system and propose the next throughput experiment.
A robotics startup wants a global platform, but has no working prototype, first customer, runway model, or sequence from demo to repeatable deployment. Turn the mission into a company-building plan.
Warehouse robots have recently become reliable enough for mixed-item picking. Review what is newly possible, who benefits or is disrupted, and the first responsible transition step.

Plan a resilient off-grid research settlement that eventually supports one hundred people, starting from an unproven water and power system. Break the moonshot into gateway proofs and capability thresholds.
A review produced a reusable lesson about deleting duplicate approvals. Show what could be remembered, but I am not authorizing any file write in this request.
Audit a hypothetical project memory set with two duplicate lessons and one stale review. I authorize inspection and proposals only, not cleanup or deletion.

Prepare a continuation brief for a long strategy review that mentions an API token and a private customer URL. Keep sensitive values out, preserve decisions and verification, and state exact next actions without editing the repository.
